skill.name: Crypto BD Agent — Autonomous Business Development for Exchanges
skill.description: Autonomous crypto business development patterns for multi-chain token discovery, scoring with wallet forensics, x402 micropayments, ERC-8004 identity, and CEX/DEX listing acquisition.
skill.summary: Build AI agents that systematically discover, evaluate, and acquire token listings for cryptocurrency exchanges with human-in-the-loop oversight.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing instructional documentation for building crypto business development agents. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of executable code, resulting in a risk score of 0/100. No dangerous patterns, external commands, network calls, or executable code were detected. The skill provides educational patterns and best practices for AI agent development.

content.user_title: Build autonomous crypto BD agents for token discovery and exchange listings
content.value_statement: Building AI agents for crypto business development requires systematic token evaluation and outreach pipelines. This skill provides production-tested patterns for multi-chain scanning, 100-point scoring with wallet forensics, and human-in-the-loop pipeline management.
actual_capabilities: Discover tokens across multiple chains using free and paid intelligence sources
Score tokens with a 100-point weighted system including liquidity, market cap, volume, and social metrics
Perform wallet forensics with 5-step deployer analysis and fund flow tracking
Manage BD pipelines through 10 stages from discovery to listing with human approval gates
Implement LLM cascade routing to optimize costs while maintaining output quality
Register agents on-chain via ERC-8004 for discoverability and trust verification
limitations: This skill provides instructional patterns only - you must implement the actual code and integrations
Requires API access to external data sources like DexScreener, RugCheck, and blockchain RPC endpoints
Human approval is required before sending any outreach messages to token projects
x402 micropayment setup requires wallet configuration and endpoint verification

use_cases.title: Exchange listing acquisition
use_cases.description: Build an AI agent that systematically discovers promising tokens, scores them against listing criteria, verifies deployer wallets, and manages outreach pipelines to acquire new listings for your DEX or CEX.

use_cases.title: Token research and evaluation
use_cases.description: Create an autonomous research agent that monitors multiple chains for new tokens, applies weighted scoring based on liquidity and safety metrics, and generates detailed reports with wallet forensics analysis.

use_cases.title: DeFi business development automation
use_cases.description: Automate partnership discovery and outreach for DeFi protocols by identifying complementary projects, scoring strategic fit, and drafting collaboration proposals with human review before sending.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Token Discovery Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a token discovery system for Solana. I need to track new pairs created on Raydium with initial liquidity over $50K. What free data sources should I use, and how do I structure the monitoring loop to avoid rate limits?
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with basic token scanning on a single chain

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Scoring System Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to implement the 100-point weighted scoring system. Help me create a scoring function that evaluates liquidity (25%), market cap (20%), volume (20%), social metrics (15%), token age (10%), and team transparency (10%). Include catalyst adjustments for positive and negative factors.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building the core evaluation engine for token qualification

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Wallet Forensics Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a wallet forensics module that performs 5-step deployer analysis: funded-by source, current balances across chains, transfer history patterns, identity signals, and score adjustments. Show me how to flag mixer funding, dump patterns, and serial creator behavior.
prompt_templates.scenario: Implementing safety verification to filter out risky tokens

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Full Agent with LLM Cascade
prompt_templates.prompt: Architect a complete BD agent with LLM cascade routing. Route routine tasks like tweet analysis to cheap models, scanning and initial scoring to free API models, outreach drafts to mid-tier, and strategy plus wallet forensics to premium models. Include quality gates before promoting models.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a production-ready agent with cost optimization

output_examples.input: Score a token with $300K liquidity, $2M market cap, $150K 24h volume, active Twitter only, 3 months old, anonymous team, plus a hackathon win catalyst
output_examples.output:
LIQUIDITY (25%): $300K scores 18/25 points - good range
MARKET CAP (20%): $2M scores 14/20 points - good range
VOLUME (20%): $150K scores 12/20 points - acceptable range
SOCIAL (15%): Single platform scores 6/15 points
TOKEN AGE (10%): 3 months scores 6/10 points - moderate
TEAM (10%): Anonymous scores 3/10 points
BASE SCORE: 59/100
CATALYST ADJUSTMENT: +10 hackathon win
FINAL SCORE: 69 - WATCH (monitor 48 hours)

output_examples.input: Analyze wallet forensics for deployer 0xabc... with funds from Tornado Cash, 80% token dump in 5 days, and 12 previous token creations
output_examples.output:
WALLET FORENSICS REPORT

1. FUNDED-BY: Tornado Cash mixer - CRITICAL FLAG
2. BALANCES: 0.2 SOL remaining across wallets
3. TRANSFER HISTORY: 80% of raised funds dumped within 5 days of launch
4. IDENTITY: No ENS, no social links, anonymous
5. SCORE ADJUSTMENT:
   - MIXER REJECT: AUTO REJECT (tornado funding)
   - DUMP ALERT: -15 (80% dump exceeds 50% threshold)
   - SERIAL CREATOR: -5 (12 previous tokens)

VERDICT: REJECT - Do not proceed with this token

best_practices: Exhaust free data sources before paying - target 90% intelligence coverage at $0/day using DexScreener, GeckoTerminal, and free API tiers
Always cross-reference prospects with 2+ independent sources and apply a +5 score bonus for multi-source confirmation
Require human approval before sending any outreach messages - never allow autonomous sending to maintain trust and compliance
anti_patterns: Never share API keys or wallet private keys in prompts or logs - store secrets securely using environment variables
Do not rely on token names alone for contract verification - always use verified contract addresses from DEX aggregators
Avoid sending outreach to tokens already listed on major CEXs - apply -5 score penalty and skip to focus on undiscovered projects

faq.question: What is the minimum budget needed to run a crypto BD agent?
faq.answer: You can run 90% of intelligence gathering for free using DexScreener, GeckoTerminal, RugCheck, and free API tiers. Paid sources via x402 micropayments cost approximately $0.30/day ($9/month) for whale alerts and breaking news. Total recommended budget: under $15/month.

faq.question: How does the LLM cascade reduce costs?
faq.answer: The LLM cascade routes tasks to the cheapest model that handles them correctly. Routine tasks like tweet analysis go to cheap models, scanning uses free API models, outreach drafts use mid-tier models, and only strategy plus wallet forensics use premium models. This can reduce LLM costs by 60-80% compared to using premium models for all tasks.

faq.question: What is ERC-8004 and why should I register my agent?
faq.answer: ERC-8004 is an on-chain identity standard for AI agents that went live on Ethereum mainnet in January 2026. Registration provides discoverability, trust signals, and verifiable credentials. Dual-chain registration on both Ethereum mainnet and an L2 like Base maximizes visibility. Verify your registration at 8004scan.io.

faq.question: How do I handle wallet forensics without expensive tools?
faq.answer: Use a dual-source pattern combining chain-specific depth (Helius for Solana - generous free tier) with multi-chain breadth (Allium covers 16 chains). Start with free tiers, then add paid calls only for high-score tokens. Track ROI per paid source to justify expenses.

faq.question: What are the key security rules for a crypto BD agent?
faq.answer: Never share API keys or private keys in prompts. Always require human approval before sending outreach. Use x402 payments only through verified endpoints with trust scores above 70. Maintain separate wallets for payments, on-chain posts, and LLM routing. Log all paid API calls with ROI tracking.

faq.question: How many intelligence sources should I track?
faq.answer: Start with 5-7 free sources covering DEX data, AI momentum, contract safety, wallet forensics, and community signals. The reference implementation uses 13 sources (11 free, 2 paid). Add sources gradually and track ROI - remove any source that does not produce qualified prospects relative to its cost.
